title: IT Support Engineer
how_to_become: To become an IT support engineer, you'll need an HND in Computing: Technical Support or an equivalent qualification at SCQF level 8.

salary.entry_note: IT support engineers in the NHS start at £30,353 before tax.
salary.experienced_note: IT support engineers in the NHS can earn up to £41,424 before tax.

description: In the NHS, IT support engineers help to maintain digital devices, services, and systems across the health service. They provide front-line support and advice to make sure technical problems with hardware, software, and computer networks are resolved at first contact.
role_overview: IT service desk technicians provide support to people who need help with their computer hardware or software. They are vital to the running of the health service.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education is needed, along with an HND in Computing: Technical Support or equivalent at SCQF level 8.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to university, college, or a new job with NHSScotland. You'll get training, increase your knowledge, and learn new skills.

college_courses: HND Computing: Technical Support at SCQF level 8
HND Technologies in Business at SCQF level 8

learning_and_development.overview: You will be encouraged to develop your career in the NHS. As well as learning specific to your role, you'll need to complete mandatory training.
learning_and_development.note: Your NHS employer will confirm their mandatory training requirements during your induction.

career_progression.overview: Gaining new qualifications will help when applying for more senior roles.

chunks.text: To become an IT support engineer in the NHS, you'll need an HND in Computing: Technical Support or an equivalent qualification at SCQF level 8.
IT support engineers in the NHS start at £30,353 before tax and can earn up to £41,424 before tax at experienced level.

NHS IT support engineers maintain digital devices, services, and systems across the health service. They provide front-line support to resolve technical problems with hardware, software, and computer networks at first contact.
IT service desk technicians support staff with computer hardware and software issues and are vital to the running of the health service.

Useful school subjects include Computing Science, Engineering Science, Administration and IT, Physics, English, and Maths. An HND in Computing: Technical Support or equivalent at SCQF level 8 is required.
Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to university, college, or a new job with NHSScotland.

Modern Apprenticeships in IT and Telecommunications or Digital Applications are available. College qualifications include HND Computing: Technical Support at SCQF level 8 and HND Technologies in Business at SCQF level 8.

Mandatory training for NHS IT support engineers includes health and safety, occupational health, fire safety, counter-fraud, moving and handling, and safe information handling. Requirements are confirmed during induction.
Further qualifications such as ITIL Foundation Certificate and ITIL Practitioner Certificate support progression into roles like IT systems support specialist or IT business analyst.
